_meta.title: Prop-gated container reflow for ui-row/ui-column — the Gen-UI no-CSS-consumer lock (feeds ADR-0096)
_meta.author: system-planner
_meta.note: DECOMPOSE mode (design intake for one ADR; not a build plan — leaves carry no accept predicates). OUTSIDE-IN = the design surface the ADR must cover; INSIDE-OUT = the needs the reproduced card-game bug + the four dispatch questions name, derived from the bug report and the source reads, independent of the structure.

nodes.label: component-layer lock mechanism (row/column reflow prop + attribute-guarded @container rules + per-tag defaults)
catalog surface (catalog.json Row/Column reflow rows; accessorFactory 1:1 pass-through)
model reachability (ADR-0071 derived inventory + optional ADR-0091 mini-skill idiom module)
blast-radius disposition (site pages enumerated; test re-keys named; app-shell/list/grid excluded)
corpus/exemplar disposition (11 shipped records; 8 emit Column)
lifecycle wiring (Extends ADR-0016 two-way link + README index row, proposed status)

actions.label: pin a Column's vertical stacking regardless of ancestor container width, reachable with props alone (the forcing need — the reproduced card-game flip)
pin a Row's horizontal direction with props alone (the toolbar/segmented-cluster case under a cramped container)
preserve the ADR-0016 cl.4 container-query switching capability as an explicit, reachable option (capability kept, not deleted)
make a prop-only composer safe BY DEFAULT — correctness must not depend on the model knowing to set a prop
surface the new prop + its idiom to the live model (inventory advertisement; idiom-level guidance where needed)
keep every currently-shipped site page rendering as designed (verify nothing relies on the changed default)
keep the shipped corpus exemplars rendering as authored (they are prop-only consumers too)
record the decision per the log lifecycle: an extension of still-standing ADR-0016, proposed not self-ratified, indexed, back-linked

edges.why: the catalog rows mirror the component's reflected prop schema (enum members + per-tag default-first order) — the component layer pins what the catalog may declare (ADR-0076 enum honoring keys off it)
the ADR-0071 system prompt derives its component/prop inventory from catalog.json at run time — the prop must exist in the catalog before the inventory can advertise it
the blast-radius disposition enumerates consumers of the SPECIFIC default being changed — it can only be assessed against the chosen mechanism + defaults
whether exemplars need edits depends on the default: a default-locked column renders the shipped records as authored with zero corpus churn; an opt-in lock would force record edits
the index row and the ADR-0016 Extended-by back-link cite the numbered record — it must exist with its final number/title first
